article_title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its First Public Mythos-Class Model, With Sensitive Queries Routed to Opus 4.8
summary: Approved with one correction: a TechCrunch-attributed quote substitutes "getting" for the source's "becoming" in an Analysis-section paraphrase inside quote marks. All other claims, specifics, pricing, routing details, and the Truell/Rodriguez quote attributions verified verbatim against the four 200-OK source snapshots.

content_preview.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its First Public Mythos-Class Model, With Sensitive Queries Routed to Opus 4.8
content_preview.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, a public version of its restricted Mythos model that falls back to Opus 4.8 on high-risk topics in under 5% of sessions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Anthropic on June 9 launched Claude Fable 5, which it describes as "a Mythos-class model that we've made safe for general use," [according to Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5). The release brings to the public the model family that began as the leaked, much-debated Claude Mythos — first surfaced through [an Anthropic data leak in March](/article/2026-03/29-anthropic-data-leak-reveals-claude-mythos-a-new-ai-model-the-company-says-poses-unprecedented-c...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured News piece (746 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis arc. Every substantive claim carries an inline source link. Quotes are confined to short, attributed spans rather than block-quoting the announcement.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four sources read from disk snapshots (no archive fallback; all status 200). source-0 anthropic.com (PRIMARY): verified verbatim — 'a Mythos-class model that we've made safe for general use', 'exceed those of any model we've ever made generally available', 'state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks...software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research', 'work autonomously for longer than any previous Claude models', the Opus 4.8 fallback sentence, 'less than 5% of sessions', '$10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens', 'available everywhere today', the Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise free-through-June-22 line, the claude-fable-5 model id, 'Project Glasswing', 'upgrade to Claude Mythos Preview'. QUOTE-ATTRIBUTION CHECK PASSED: the Michael Truell quote ('the state of the art model on CursorBench' / 'opened up a class of long-horizon problems that were out of reach for earlier models') appears verbatim attributed to Michael Truell, CEO and Co-founder, in the announcement's quote carousel; the Mario Rodriguez quote ('a real step forward for the developers GitHub serves') appears verbatim attributed to Mario Rodriguez, Chief Product Officer. The source cite elements label name+title only; affiliation is anchored by the quote content ('CursorBench' -> Cursor; 'the developers GitHub serves' -> GitHub). The article's affiliations (Cursor / GitHub) are correct and not misattributed. source-2 aws.amazon.com: verified verbatim — 'Harmful prompts related to cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, and health fall back to receive a response from Opus 4.8 instead', the 'you pay only Opus prices' billing line, 'The same model without these limits is Claude Mythos 5...small group of vetted customers', 'available today on Amazon Bedrock' in US East (N. Virginia) and Europe (Stockholm). source-3 macrumors.com: verified verbatim — 'included in Pro, Max, Team, and seat-based Enterprise plans from today until June 22'. source-1 techcrunch.com: the RSI warning quote ('advancing so rapidly that they may soon achieve recursive self-improvement (RSI), autonomously improving themselves without human intervention') and 'double the price of Opus 4.8' and 'the first publicly available version of its Mythos model' all verified verbatim. ONE DISCREPANCY: the quoted phrase 'days after warning AI is getting too dangerous' attributed to TechCrunch does not appear with that wording in the body or headline; the snapshot's TechCrunch headline is 'Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today', and the only 'too dangerous' occurrence is in the page's og social-description meta tag, which reads 'days after warning AI is becoming too dangerous'. The article substitutes 'getting' for the source's 'becoming' inside quote marks.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics (June 9 launch date, $10/$50 pricing, double-Opus-price framing, <5% routing rate, the four harmful-topic categories, Opus-pricing-on-fallback, Bedrock regions, free-through-June-22 window, Project Glasswing, Mythos Preview upgrade, claude-fable-5 identifier) trace to a cited source and match verbatim. The single inexactness is the one-word paraphrase noted above. Headline, summary, and Overview lead rest on the Anthropic primary source and the TechCrunch 'first publicly available' line — none rest on a bot-blocked source (no CNBC/NBC/SD Times present; all four cited sources returned 200).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and restrained. Analysis section labels capability/benchmark claims as Anthropic's own and adds a 'What We Don't Know' caveat on the absence of independent third-party evaluations. No AI self-reference, no sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No duplication — no prior 'Fable 5' article exists in the archive. Despite heavy prior Mythos coverage, the piece centers on what is genuinely new (the June 9 public GA of Fable 5, the Opus 4.8 fallback routing, pricing, the restricted Mythos 5 / Project Glasswing tier) and uses prior coverage only as two brief cross-reference links rather than re-narrating it. Both internal cross-references resolve: /article/2026-03/29-anthropic-data-leak-reveals-claude-mythos-... and /article/2026-04/20-anthropic-releases-claude-opus-47-... both exist on disk. The force-follow-up bar (center on new substance, cross-reference rather than re-narrate prior coverage) is met.
concerns: TechCrunch-attributed quote 'days after warning AI is getting too dangerous' substitutes 'getting' for the source's 'becoming' ('...AI is becoming too dangerous', from TechCrunch's og social-description meta). Minor single-word paraphrase inside quote marks, in the Analysis section (not headline/summary/lead). Filed as a correction; the underlying framing is accurate and sourced.
recommendations: When quoting an outlet's framing, prefer the body/headline wording over the social-share meta description, and reproduce it word-for-word.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced News report on the Claude Fable 5 public launch. All four sources read from their committed snapshots; every specific and both partner quotes verified verbatim with correct speaker/title/outlet attribution. One recoverable issue — a single-word substitution inside a TechCrunch-attributed quote in the Analysis section — is honestly summarizable in a corrections note and does not touch the headline, summary, or lead. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
